mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If the month-over-month growth rate of RSAFS does not correlate positively with SPX 5-day returns (Rank IC < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) remains near zero, it signals an accommodative monetary policy, reducing the cost of capital and encouraging investment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a medium horizon.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% for a sustained period (e.g., 3 consecutive months).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling unemployment (UNRATE) and rising payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a strengthening labor market, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which positively impacts corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns over a medium horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by > 0.1pp in 1 month AND PAYEMS rises > 100k in 1 month
failure_condition: If periods where UNRATE falls by >0.1pp and PAYEMS rises >100k simultaneously do not consistently lead to positive SPX 20-day returns (accuracy < 55%).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic expansion, increasing industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust manufacturing activity and corporate profitability, driving long-term S&P 500 growth over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth above 0.5% MoM does not lead to positive SPX 60-day returns during confirmed expansionary periods (e.g., NBER-defined expansions).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained high levels of housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong investment in residential construction and related industries, signaling broader economic health and supporting S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If housing starts consistently above 1500k units do not precede positive SPX 60-day returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes purchasing power and can lead to tighter monetary policy, increasing discount rates and negatively impacting equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day returns are not negative on average when year-over-year CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 2.5% for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals weakening labor market conditions and potential economic contraction, leading to reduced consumer spending and lower corporate profits, negatively impacting S&P 500 returns over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:historical correlation of unemployment spikes with recessions
failure_condition: If a monthly increase in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points does not consistently precede negative SPX 5-day returns in at least 65% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is strong but accompanied by very low interest rates (FEDFUNDS), it suggests an economy with significant growth potential still supported by accommodative policy, leading to strong S&P 500 returns over a long horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:post-recession growth often supported by low rates
failure_condition: If periods of strong GDP growth combined with low FEDFUNDS do not consistently result in positive SPX 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.08) over a 5-year rolling window.
